content.title: LAND OF THE RISING SUM
content.summary: {'A': '日本在数学成绩方面的平均水平明显优于英格兰和威尔士，尽管两国在教育经费上的支出相似，但日本的数学成绩更高且更一致，这是如何实现的？', 'B': '日本的初中学校分为三个学年，从七年级（13岁）到九年级（15岁），几乎所有学生都在公立学校就读，只有3%在私立学校。学校通常设计现代化，远离道路，内部宽敞。教室很大，学生们坐在单人桌子上排成一排。课程标准化，每节课50分钟，之后有10分钟的休息时间，让学生们放松一下。老师会进行正式致辞和互相鞠躬，然后进行整班教学。班级人数通常较多，大约40人，没有分班。学生在整个学校的所有课程中都待在同一个班级，形成了较强的班级认同和忠诚。学生就读自己所在社区的学校，理论上消除了学校排名。实际上，在东京由于学校相对集中，有一些竞争争取进入某个地区的“更好”的学校。', 'C': '这段话主要讲述了传统的教学方式作为课程的基础，学生们静静地做笔记，教材由中央教育机构Monbusho提供，这些教材小巧、逻辑清晰，除了批准教材外，Monbusho还决定高度集中的国家课程及其传授方式。', 'D': '这段话主要讲述了日本学校课堂的教学模式，包括学生在黑板上展示作业解答，老师进行评价和纠正，学生自己批改作业，老师讲解课题并进行示范，学生进行个人练习，老师巡视确保学生掌握情况。', 'E': '这段话主要讲述了在日本的教育系统中，大型、能力不同的班级可以在6岁至15岁的义务教育期间一直保持在一起学习数学，并且教师会在课后或课程结束时给予个别帮助，如果需要的话还会布置额外的作业。在课堂上，老师会帮助有困难的学生，或者学生会悄悄向邻座求助。通过培养班级认同感，学生们热衷于互相帮助，因为他们的进步与班级的进步息息相关。然而，这种帮助似乎不足以让学习较慢的学生跟上进度。然而，日本对教育的态度是“只要你努力，几乎可以做到任何事情”。家长会密切关注孩子的进步，并在帮助孩子跟上班级进度方面发挥作用，如果需要额外的帮助，会送他们去上“塾”（私人晚间补习班），并鼓励他们更加努力学习。这种方式似乎有效，至少对于学校中95%的学生来说。', 'F': '日本数学教学成功的主要因素包括：重视教育、将数学视为重要的必修科目、注重努力和准确性；班级对较慢学生的支持态度、缺乏竞争氛围、积极强调自主学习和提高自己水平；而有关日本课堂的重复乏味和死记硬背的观点可能是不公正和不合理的，观察到的数学课大多数是好的，有一两节课更是激发人心的。'}

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: Japan has a significantly better record in terms of average mathematical attainment than England and Wales. Large sample international comparisons of pupils' attainments since the 1960s have established that not only did Japanese pupils at age 13 have better scores of average attainment, but there was also a larger proportion of 'low' attainers in England, where, incidentally, the variation in attainment scores was much greater. The percentage of Gross National Product spent on education is reasonably similar in the two countries, so how is this higher and more consistent attainment in maths achieved?

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text:
Lower secondary schools in Japan cover three school years, from the seventh grade (age 13) to the ninth grade (age 15). Virtually all pupils at this stage attend state schools: only 3 per cent are in the private sector. Schools are usually modern in design, set well back from the road and spacious inside. Classrooms are large and pupils sit at single desks in rows. Lessons last for a standardised 50 minutes and are always followed by a 10-minute break, which gives the pupils a chance to let off steam. Teachers begin with a formal address and mutual bowing, and then concentrate on whole-class teaching.
Classes are large - usually about 40 - and are unstreamed. Pupils stay in the same class for all lessons throughout the school and develop considerable class identity and loyalty. Pupils attend the school in their own neighbourhood, which in theory removes ranking by school. In practice in Tokyo, because of the relative concentration of schools, there is some competition to get into the 'better' school in a particular area.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: Traditional ways of teaching form the basis of the lesson and the remarkably quiet classes take their own notes of the points made and the examples demonstrated. Everyone has their own copy of the textbook supplied by the central education authority, Monbusho, as part of the concept of free compulsory education up to the age of 15. These textbooks are, on the whole, small, presumably inexpensive to produce, but well set out and logically developed. (One teacher was particularly keen to introduce colour and pictures into maths textbooks: he felt this would make them more accessible to pupils brought up in a cartoon culture.) Besides approving textbooks, Monbusho also decides the highly centralised national curriculum and how it is to be delivered.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: Lessons all follow the same pattern. At the beginning, the pupils put solutions to the homework on the board, then the teachers comment, correct or elaborate as necessary. Pupils mark their own homework: this is an important principle in Japanese schooling as it enables pupils to see where and why they made a mistake, so that these can be avoided in future. No one minds mistakes or ignorance as long as you are prepared to learn from them. After the homework has been discussed, the teacher explains the topic of the lesson, slowly and with a lot of repetition and elaboration. Examples are demonstrated on the board; questions from the textbook are worked through first with the class, and then the class is set questions from the textbook to do individually. Only rarely are supplementary worksheets distributed in a maths class. The impression is that the logical nature of the textbooks and their comprehensive coverage of different types of examples, combined with the relative homogeneity of the class, renders worksheets unnecessary. At this point, the teacher would circulate and make sure that all the pupils were coping well.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text:
It is remarkable that large, mixed-ability classes could be kept together for maths throughout all their compulsory schooling from 6 to 15. Teachers say that they give individual help at the end of a lesson or after school, setting extra work if necessary. In observed lessons, any strugglers would be assisted by the teacher or quietly seek help from their neighbour. Carefully fostered class identity makes pupils keen to help each other - anyway, it is in their interests since the class progresses together.
This scarcely seems adequate help to enable slow learners to keep up. However, the Japanese attitude towards education runs along the lines of 'if you work hard enough, you can do almost anything'. Parents are kept closely informed of their children's progress and will play a part in helping their children to keep up with class, sending them to 'Juku' (private evening tuition) if extra help is needed and encouraging them to work harder. It seems to work, at least for 95 per cent of the school population.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text:
So what are the major contributing factors in the success of maths teaching? Clearly, attitudes are important. Education is valued greatly in Japanese culture; maths is recognised as an important compulsory subject throughout schooling; and the emphasis is on hard work coupled with a focus on accuracy.
Other relevant points relate to the supportive attitude of a class towards slower pupils, the lack of competition within a class, and the positive emphasis on learning for oneself and improving one's own standard. And the view of repetitively boring lessons and learning the facts by heart, which is sometimes quoted in relation to Japanese classes, may be unfair and unjustified. No poor maths lessons were observed. They were mainly good and one or two were inspirational.

groups.title: List of Headings
groups.desc:
Questions 1-5

The text has 6 sections. 
Choose the correct heading for each section and move it into the gap.

Example Answer
Paragraph A iv - Research findings concerning achievements in maths

list.text: Research findings concerning achievements in maths
The typical format of a maths lesson
Background to middle-years education in Japan
The key to Japanese successes in maths education

questions.analysis: 这段文字主要介绍了日本初中教育的基本情况，包括年级范围、学校类型、班级设置等。段落的核心在于提供日本初中教育的背景信息，与小标题vii "Background to middle-years education in Japan" 完全匹配。因此，最适合的标题是vii，而不是其他选项。

central_sentences.sentence: Lower secondary schools in Japan cover three school years, from the seventh grade (age 13) to the ninth grade (age 15).
central_sentences.translation: 日本的初级中学包括三个学年，从七年级（13岁）到九年级（15岁）。

syn_ary.synonym: Lower secondary schools in Japan cover three school years, from the seventh grade (age 13) to the ninth grade (age 15)

target_sen.target_sen: Lower secondary schools in Japan cover three school years, from the seventh grade (age 13) to the ninth grade (age 15).
Lower secondary schools in Japan cover three school years, from the seventh grade (age 13) to the ninth grade (age 15).

questions.analysis: 这段文字的主要内容是关于 Monbusho（日本文部省）对教材和课程的影响。文中明确提到 Monbusho 批准教材并决定国家课程，这些都是对教育的直接影响。因此，最合适的小标题是 i: The influence of Monbusho。

central_sentences.sentence: Besides approving textbooks, Monbusho also decides the highly centralised national curriculum and how it is to be delivered.
central_sentences.translation: 除了批准教科书外，文部省还决定高度集中的国家课程及其实施方式。

target_sen.target_sen: Besides approving textbooks, Monbusho also decides the highly centralised national curriculum and how it is to be delivered.

syn_ary.synonym: decides the highly centralised national curriculum

target_sen.target_sen: Besides approving textbooks, Monbusho also decides the highly centralised national curriculum and how it is to be delivered.
Everyone has their own copy of the textbook supplied by the central education authority, Monbusho, as part of the concept of free compulsory education up to the age of 15.
Besides approving textbooks, Monbusho also decides the highly centralised national curriculum and how it is to be delivered.
Everyone has their own copy of the textbook supplied by the central education authority, Monbusho, as part of the concept of free compulsory education up to the age of 15.

questions.analysis: Section D详细描述了数学课的标准流程，包括作业展示和讨论、老师讲解、问题示范、学生独立完成题目等。这些步骤构成了数学课的典型格式。因此，标题v: The typical format of a maths lesson是最合适的。

central_sentences.sentence: Lessons all follow the same pattern.
central_sentences.translation: 所有课程都遵循相同的模式。

target_sen.target_sen: Lessons all follow the same pattern.
Only rarely are supplementary worksheets distributed in a maths class.

questions.analysis: Section E的主要内容是描述如何帮助那些学习较慢的学生。文章提到老师在课后或放学后给予个别帮助，布置额外作业，以及同学之间的互助。这些内容都与小标题ii: Helping less successful students的意思完全一致。因此，Section E对应的小标题是ii。

central_sentences.sentence: Teachers say that they give individual help at the end of a lesson or after school, setting extra work if necessary.
central_sentences.translation: 教师表示，他们会在课后或放学后给予个别辅导，必要时会布置额外作业。

central_sentences.sentence: Parents are kept closely informed of their children's progress and will play a part in helping their children to keep up with class, sending them to 'Juku' (private evening tuition) if extra help is needed and encouraging them to work harder.
central_sentences.translation: 家长会密切关注孩子的学习进展，并在帮助孩子跟上班级进度方面发挥作用，如果需要额外帮助，他们会送孩子去"Juku"（私人晚间辅导），并鼓励他们更加努力学习。

In observed lessons, any strugglers would be assisted by the teacher or quietly seek help from their neighbour.
Carefully fostered class identity makes pupils keen to help each other - anyway, it is in their interests since the class progresses together.
This scarcely seems adequate help to enable slow learners to keep up.

questions.analysis: Section F主要讨论了日本数学教育成功的关键因素，并总结了相关因素。这与viii: The key to Japanese successes in maths education最为契合。文章明确提到“the major contributing factors in the success of maths teaching”，这与标题中的“the key to Japanese successes in maths education”是同义表达。

central_sentences.sentence: So what are the major contributing factors in the success of maths teaching?
central_sentences.translation: 那么，数学教学成功的主要贡献因素是什么？

groups.desc:
Questions 6-9

Do the following statements agree with the claims of the writer in Reading Passage 1?
Choose
YES if the statement agrees with the claims of the writer,
NO if the statement contradicts the claims of the writer,
NOT GIVEN if it is impossible to say what the writer thinks about this.

questions.title: There is a wider range of achievement amongst English pupils studying maths than amongst their Japanese counterparts.
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是YES。题干中的“wider range of achievement”在原文中对应的是“variation in attainment scores was much greater”，而“English pupils”对应的是“in England”，“Japanese counterparts”对应的是“Japanese pupils”。原文明确指出，英格兰的成就分数变化范围更大，这与题干的陈述一致，因此答案是YES。

central_sentences.sentence: Large sample international comparisons of pupils' attainments since the 1960s have established that not only did Japanese pupils at age 13 have better scores of average attainment, but there was also a larger proportion of 'low' attainers in England, where, incidentally, the variation in attainment scores was much greater.
central_sentences.translation: 自20世纪60年代以来的大规模国际学生成绩比较表明，不仅13岁的日本学生的平均成绩更好，而且英格兰的"低"成绩学生比例更大，顺便说一下，英格兰的成绩变化范围也更大。

target_sen.target_sen: Large sample international comparisons of pupils' attainments since the 1960s have established that not only did Japanese pupils at age 13 have better scores of average attainment, but there was also a larger proportion of 'low' attainers in England, where, incidentally, the variation in attainment scores was much greater.
Japan has a significantly better record in terms of average mathematical attainment than England and Wales.

Large sample international comparisons of pupils' attainments since the 1960s have established that not only did Japanese pupils at age 13 have better scores of average attainment, but there was also a larger proportion of 'low' attainers in England, where, incidentally, the variation in attainment scores was much greater.
The percentage of Gross National Product spent on education is reasonably similar in the two countries, so how is this higher and more consistent attainment in maths achieved?

Large sample international comparisons of pupils' attainments since the 1960s have established that not only did Japanese pupils at age 13 have better scores of average attainment, but there was also a larger proportion of 'low' attainers in England, where, incidentally, the variation in attainment scores was much greater.

Large sample international comparisons of pupils' attainments since the 1960s have established that not only did Japanese pupils at age 13 have better scores of average attainment, but there was also a larger proportion of 'low' attainers in England, where, incidentally, the variation in attainment scores was much greater.

questions.title: The percentage of Gross National Product spent on education generally reflects the level of attainment in mathematics.
questions.analysis: 题干的意思是教育经费占国民生产总值的百分比通常反映数学成绩水平。而原文中明确指出，尽管日本和英格兰及威尔士在教育经费占国民生产总值的百分比上相似，但日本的数学成绩更高且更一致。这说明教育经费百分比并不能反映数学成绩水平。因此，题干的说法是错误的，正确答案是 NO。

central_sentences.sentence: The percentage of Gross National Product spent on education is reasonably similar in the two countries, so how is this higher and more consistent attainment in maths achieved?
central_sentences.translation: 两国用于教育的国民生产总值百分比相当接近，那么日本是如何达到更高且更一致的数学成绩的呢？

target_sen.target_sen: The percentage of Gross National Product spent on education is reasonably similar in the two countries, so how is this higher and more consistent attainment in maths achieved?
The percentage of Gross National Product spent on education is reasonably similar in the two countries, so how is this higher and more consistent attainment in maths achieved?
The percentage of Gross National Product spent on education is reasonably similar in the two countries, so how is this higher and more consistent attainment in maths achieved?

questions.title: Private schools in Japan are more modern and spacious than state-run lower secondary schools.
questions.analysis: 题干要求比较日本的私立学校和公立初中的现代和宽敞程度，而文章中只提到公立学校的现代和宽敞，没有提到私立学校的情况。因此，无法判断私立学校是否比公立学校更现代和宽敞，答案为“NOT GIVEN”。

central_sentences.sentence: Virtually all pupils at this stage attend state schools: only 3 per cent are in the private sector. Schools are usually modern in design, set well back from the road and spacious inside.
central_sentences.translation: 在这个阶段，几乎所有的学生都就读于公立学校：只有3%的学生在私立学校就读。学校通常设计现代，远离道路，内部宽敞。

target_sen.target_sen: Virtually all pupils at this stage attend state schools: only 3 per cent are in the private sector.
Virtually all pupils at this stage attend state schools: only 3 per cent are in the private sector.
Schools are usually modern in design, set well back from the road and spacious inside.
Schools are usually modern in design, set well back from the road and spacious inside.

questions.title: Teachers mark homework in Japanese schools.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息是“老师批改作业”，而原文明确指出是“学生自己批改作业”。因此，题干中的信息与原文内容相反，正确答案是 NO。通过识别题干中的关键词（Teachers, mark, homework）并在原文中找到对应的句子，可以确定答案。

central_sentences.sentence: Pupils mark their own homework: this is an important principle in Japanese schooling as it enables pupils to see where and why they made a mistake, so that these can be avoided in future.
central_sentences.translation: 学生自己批改作业：这是日本学校教育中的一个重要原则，因为它使学生能够看到他们在哪里犯了错误以及为什么犯错，从而在将来避免这些错误。

target_sen.target_sen: Pupils mark their own homework: this is an important principle in Japanese schooling as it enables pupils to see where and why they made a mistake, so that these can be avoided in future.

questions.title: Maths textbooks in Japanese schools are

options.text: well organised and adapted to the needs of the pupils.
written to be used in conjunction with TV programmes.
not very popular with many Japanese teachers.

questions.analysis: 正确答案是B，因为文章明确提到课本“well set out and logically developed”，这与B选项的“well organised and adapted to the needs of the pupils”意思相近。文章描述了课本的设计和逻辑性，这表明课本是组织良好且适应学生需求的。

central_sentences.sentence: These textbooks are, on the whole, small, presumably inexpensive to produce, but well set out and logically developed.
central_sentences.translation: 这些课本总体上比较小，推测生产成本较低，但设计良好且逻辑清晰。

target_sen.target_sen: These textbooks are, on the whole, small, presumably inexpensive to produce, but well set out and logically developed.
(One teacher was particularly keen to introduce colour and pictures into maths textbooks: he felt this would make them more accessible to pupils brought up in a cartoon culture. )
Besides approving textbooks, Monbusho also decides the highly centralised national curriculum and how it is to be delivered.

questions.title: When a new maths topic is introduced,

options.text: students answer questions on the board.
students rely entirely on the textbook.
it is carefully and patiently explained to the students.
it is usual for students to use extra worksheets.

questions.analysis: 文章中明确提到老师在介绍新课题时，会“慢慢地、并且反复详细地讲解”，这与选项C“它会被仔细而耐心地解释给学生”直接对应。因此，正确答案是C。

central_sentences.sentence: After the homework has been discussed, the teacher explains the topic of the lesson, slowly and with a lot of repetition and elaboration.
central_sentences.translation: 在讨论完作业后，教师会慢慢地、反复详细地解释课程主题。

target_sen.target_sen: After the homework has been discussed, the teacher explains the topic of the lesson, slowly and with a lot of repetition and elaboration.

questions.title: How do schools deal with students who experience difficulties?

options.text: They are given appropriate supplementary tuition.
They are encouraged to copy from other pupils.
They are forced to explain their slow progress.
They are placed in a mixed-ability class.

questions.analysis: 正确答案是A，因为文章明确提到有困难的学生会被送去“Juku”（私人晚间补习），这与选项A中的“appropriate supplementary tuition”是同义表达。

central_sentences.sentence: Parents are kept closely informed of their children's progress and will play a part in helping their children to keep up with class, sending them to 'Juku' (private evening tuition) if extra help is needed and encouraging them to work harder.
central_sentences.translation: 家长们会被密切告知孩子们的学习进展，并会参与帮助孩子们跟上班级进度，如果需要额外帮助，会送他们去"Juku"（私人晚间补习），并鼓励他们更加努力。

target_sen.target_sen: In observed lessons, any strugglers would be assisted by the teacher or quietly seek help from their neighbour.
This scarcely seems adequate help to enable slow learners to keep up.
In observed lessons, any strugglers would be assisted by the teacher or quietly seek help from their neighbour.
Teachers say that they give individual help at the end of a lesson or after school, setting extra work if necessary.
In observed lessons, any strugglers would be assisted by the teacher or quietly seek help from their neighbour.
Carefully fostered class identity makes pupils keen to help each other - anyway, it is in their interests since the class progresses together.
This scarcely seems adequate help to enable slow learners to keep up.

Parents are kept closely informed of their children's progress and will play a part in helping their children to keep up with class, sending them to 'Juku' (private evening tuition) if extra help is needed and encouraging them to work harder.

questions.title: Why do Japanese students tend to achieve relatively high rates of success in maths?

options.text: It is a compulsory subject in Japan.
They are used to working without help from others.
Much effort is made and correct answers are emphasised.
There is a strong emphasis on repetitive learning.

questions.analysis: 正确答案是C：Much effort is made and correct answers are emphasised。文章中多次提到日本学生在数学上取得成功的原因是他们付出了很多努力，并且重视正确答案。例如，文章提到“if you work hard enough, you can do almost anything”和“the positive emphasis on learning for oneself and improving one's own standard”。这些都表明努力和正确答案的重要性。

central_sentences.sentence: So what are the major contributing factors in the success of maths teaching? Clearly, attitudes are important. Education is valued greatly in Japanese culture; maths is recognised as an important compulsory subject throughout schooling; and the emphasis is on hard work coupled with a focus on accuracy.
central_sentences.translation: 那么，数学教学成功的主要贡献因素是什么呢？显然，态度很重要。在日本文化中，教育受到高度重视；数学被认为是贯穿整个学校教育的重要必修科目；而且强调努力学习，同时注重准确性。

target_sen.target_sen: So what are the major contributing factors in the success of maths teaching?
So what are the major contributing factors in the success of maths teaching?
Education is valued greatly in Japanese culture; maths is recognised as an important compulsory subject throughout schooling; and the emphasis is on hard work coupled with a focus on accuracy.
No poor maths lessons were observed.

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: The continuous and reckless use of synthetic chemicals for the control of pests which pose a threat to agricultural crops and human health is proving to be counter-productive. Apart from engendering widespread ecological disorders, pesticides have contributed to the emergence of a new breed of chemical-resistant, highly lethal superbugs.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: According to a recent study by the Food and Agriculture Organisation (FAO), more than 300 species of agricultural pests have developed resistance to a wide range of potent chemicals. Not to be left behind are the disease-spreading pests, about 100 species of which have become immune to a variety of insecticides now in use.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: One glaring disadvantage of pesticides' application is that, while destroying harmful pests, they also wipe out many useful non-targeted organisms, which keep the growth of the pest population in check. This results in what agroecologists call the 'treadmill syndrome'. Because of their tremendous breeding potential and genetic diversity, many pests are known to withstand synthetic chemicals and bear offspring with a built-in resistance to pesticides.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: The havoc that the 'treadmill syndrome' can bring about is well illustrated by what happened to cotton farmers in Central America. In the early 1940s, basking in the glory of chemical-based intensive agriculture, the farmers avidly took to pesticides as a sure measure to boost crop yield. The insecticide was applied eight times a year in the mid-1940s, rising to 28 in a season in the mid-1950s, following the sudden proliferation of three new varieties of chemical-resistant pests.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: By the mid-1960s, the situation took an alarming turn with the outbreak of four more new pests, necessitating pesticide spraying to such an extent that 50% of the financial outlay on cotton production was accounted for by pesticides. In the early 1970s, the spraying frequently reached 70 times a season as the farmers were pushed to the wall by the invasion of genetically stronger insect species.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: Most of the pesticides in the market today remain inadequately tested for properties that cause cancer and mutations as well as for other adverse effects on health, says a study by United States environmental agencies. The United States National Resource Defense Council has found that DDT was the most popular of a long list of dangerous chemicals in use.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: In the face of the escalating perils from indiscriminate applications of pesticides, a more effective and ecologically sound strategy of biological control, involving the selective use of natural enemies of the pest population, is fast gaining popularity—though, as yet, it is a new field with limited potential. The advantage of biological control in contrast to other methods is that it provides a relatively low-cost, perpetual control system with a minimum of detrimental side-effects. When handled by experts, bio-control is safe, non-polluting and self-dispersing.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: The Commonwealth Institute of Biological Control (CIBC) in Bangalore, with its global network of research laboratories and field stations, is one of the most active, non-commercial research agencies engaged in pest control by setting natural predators against parasites. CIBC also serves as a clearing-house for the export and import of biological agents for pest control world-wide.

paragraphs.label: I
paragraphs.text: CIBC successfully used a seed-feeding weevil, native to Mexico, to control the obnoxious parthenium weed, known to exert devious influence on agriculture and human health in both India and Australia. Similarly the Hyderabad-based Regional Research Laboratory (RRL), supported by CIBC, is now trying out an Argentinian weevil for the eradication of water hyacinth, another dangerous weed, which has become a nuisance in many parts of the world. According to Mrs Kaiser Jamil of RRL, 'The Argentinian weevil does not attack any other plant and a pair of adult bugs could destroy the weed in 4-5 days.' CIBC is also perfecting the technique for breeding parasites that prey on 'disapene scale' insects— notorious defoliants of fruit trees in the US and India.

paragraphs.label: J
paragraphs.text: How effectively biological control can be pressed into service is proved by the following examples. In the late 1960s, when Sri Lanka's flourishing coconut groves were plagued by leaf-mining hispides, a larval parasite imported from Singapore brought the pest under control. A natural predator indigenous to India, Neodumetia sangawani, was found useful in controlling the Rhodes grass-scale insect that was devouring forage grass in many parts of the US. By using Neochetina bruchi, a beetle native to Brazil, scientists at Kerala Agricultural University freed a 12-kilometre-long canal from the clutches of the weed Salvinia molesta, popularly called 'African Payal' in Kerala. About 30,000 hectares of rice fields in Kerala are infested by this weed.

questions.title: The use of pesticides has contributed to

options.text: change in the way ecologies are classified by agroecologists.
an imbalance in many ecologies around the world.
the prevention of ecological disasters in some parts of the world.
an increase in the range of ecologies which can be usefully farmed.

questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是 B: "an imbalance in many ecologies around the world"。文章明确提到农药的使用导致了“生态失调”（ecological disorders），这与选项 B 的“不平衡”最为对应。题干中的“contributed to”在原文中对应“engendering”，而“imbalance”对应“ecological disorders”。

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: Collecting ants can be as simple as picking up stray ones and placing them in a glass jar, or as complicated as completing an exhaustive survey of all species present in an area and estimating their relative abundances. The exact method used will depend on the final purpose of the collections. For taxonomy, or classification, long series, from a single nest, which contain all castes (workers, including majors and minors, and, if present, queens and males) are desirable, to allow the determination of variation within species. For ecological studies, the most important factor is collecting identifiable samples of as many of the different species present as possible. Unfortunately, these methods are not always compatible. The taxonomist sometimes overlooks whole species in favour of those groups currently under study, while the ecologist often collects only a limited number of specimens of each species, thus reducing their value for taxonomic investigations.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: To collect as wide a range of species as possible, several methods must be used. These include hand collecting, using baits to attract the ants, ground litter sampling, and the use of pitfall traps. Hand collecting consists of searching for ants everywhere they are likely to occur. This includes on the ground, under rocks, logs or other objects on the ground, in rotten wood on the ground or on trees, in vegetation, on tree trunks and under bark. When possible, collections should be made from nests or foraging columns and at least 20 to 25 individuals collected. This will ensure that all individuals are of the same species, and so increase their value for detailed studies. Since some species are largely nocturnal, collecting should not be confined to daytime. Specimens are collected using an aspirator (often called a pooter), forceps, a fine, moistened paint brush, or fingers, if the ants are known not to sting. Individual insects are placed in plastic or glass tubes (1.5–3.0 ml capacity for small ants, 5–8 ml for larger ants) containing 75% to 95% ethanol. Plastic tubes with secure tops are better than glass because they are lighter, and do not break as easily if mishandled.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: Baits can be used to attract and concentrate foragers. This often increases the number of individuals collected and attracts species that are otherwise elusive. Sugars and meats or oils will attract different species and a range should be utilised. These baits can be placed either on the ground or on the trunks of trees or large shrubs. When placed on the ground, baits should be situated on small paper cards or other flat, light-coloured surfaces, or in test-tubes or vials. This makes it easier to spot ants and to capture them before they can escape into the surrounding leaf litter.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: Many ants are small and forage primarily in the layer of leaves and other debris on the ground. Collecting these species by hand can be difficult. One of the most successful ways to collect them is to gather the leaf litter in which they are foraging and extract the ants from it. This is most commonly done by placing leaf litter on a screen over a large funnel, often under some heat. As the leaf litter dries from above, ants (and other animals) move downward and eventually fall out the bottom and are collected in alcohol placed below the funnel. This method works especially well in rainforests and marshy areas. A method of improving the catch when using a funnel is to sift the leaf litter through a coarse screen before placing it above the funnel. This will concentrate the litter and remove larger leaves and twigs. It will also allow more litter to be sampled when using a limited number of funnels.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: The pitfall trap is another commonly used tool for collecting ants. A pitfall trap can be any small container placed in the ground with the top level with the surrounding surface and filled with a preservative. Ants are collected when they fall into the trap while foraging. The diameter of the traps can vary from about 18 mm to 10 cm and the number used can vary from a few to several hundred. The size of the traps used is influenced largely by personal preference (although larger sizes are generally better), while the number will be determined by the study being undertaken. The preservative used is usually ethylene glycol or propylene glycol, as alcohol will evaporate quickly and the traps will dry out. One advantage of pitfall traps is that they can be used to collect over a period of time with minimal maintenance and intervention. One disadvantage is that some species are not collected as they either avoid the traps or do not commonly encounter them while foraging.

groups.desc:
Questions 27-30

Do the following statements agree with the information given in Reading Passage 3?
Choose
TRUE if the statement agrees with the information given in the text,
FALSE if the statement contradicts the information,
NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this.

questions.title: Taxonomic research involves comparing members of one group of ants.
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "Taxonomic research" 对应原文中的 "For taxonomy, or classification"，而 "one group of ants" 对应原文中的 "from a single nest"。原文提到，为了分类学研究，理想的样本是来自单个巢穴的长系列样本，这些样本包含所有种类的蚂蚁，以便确定物种内的变异。这说明分类学研究确实涉及比较同一群蚂蚁的成员。因此，答案是 TRUE。

central_sentences.sentence: For taxonomy, or classification, long series, from a single nest, which contain all castes (workers, including majors and minors, and, if present, queens and males) are desirable, to allow the determination of variation within species.
central_sentences.translation: 对于分类学或分类来说，从单个巢穴获取的长系列样本是理想的，这些样本包含所有的等级（工蚁，包括大型和小型工蚁，以及如果存在的话，蚁后和雄蚁），这样可以确定物种内的变异。

target_sen.target_sen: For taxonomy, or classification, long series, from a single nest, which contain all castes (workers, including majors and minors, and, if present, queens and males) are desirable, to allow the determination of variation within species.

For taxonomy, or classification, long series, from a single nest, which contain all castes (workers, including majors and minors, and, if present, queens and males) are desirable, to allow the determination of variation within species.

For taxonomy, or classification, long series, from a single nest, which contain all castes (workers, including majors and minors, and, if present, queens and males) are desirable, to allow the determination of variation within species.

questions.title: New species of ant are frequently identified by taxonomists.
questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是NOT GIVEN，因为文章中没有提到“新物种经常被分类学家识别”这一信息。虽然文章讨论了分类学家和收集方法，但并没有提到新物种的发现频率。学生需要通过关键词定位和信息确认来判断题干信息是否在文章中出现。如果没有明确的信息支持题干陈述，那么答案应为NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.reason: 未提及“频繁鉴定新种”这一信息。
central_sentences.analysis: 原文未提分类学家是否频繁鉴定新种，仅说其可能忽略某些物种。
sentences: The taxonomist sometimes overlooks whole species in favour of those groups currently under study, while the ecologist often collects only a limited number of specimens of each species, thus reducing their value for taxonomic investigations.

central_sentences.sentence: The taxonomist sometimes overlooks whole species in favour of those groups currently under study, while the ecologist often collects only a limited number of specimens of each species, thus reducing their value for taxonomic investigations.

questions.title: Range is the key criterion for ecological collections.
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "Range is the key criterion for ecological collections" 可以通过文章中的 "For ecological studies, the most important factor is collecting identifiable samples of as many of the different species present as possible" 找到对应关系。题干中的 "Range" 对应 "as many of the different species present as possible"，而 "key criterion" 对应 "the most important factor"。因此，题干中的信息在文章中得到了明确的支持，答案为 TRUE。

central_sentences.sentence: For ecological studies, the most important factor is collecting identifiable samples of as many of the different species present as possible.
central_sentences.translation: 对于生态研究来说，最重要的因素是收集尽可能多的不同物种的可识别样本。

syn_ary.synonym: as many of the different species present as possible

target_sen.target_sen: For ecological studies, the most important factor is collecting identifiable samples of as many of the different species present as possible.
For ecological studies, the most important factor is collecting identifiable samples of as many of the different species present as possible.
For ecological studies, the most important factor is collecting identifiable samples of as many of the different species present as possible.

questions.title: A single collection of ants can generally be used for both taxonomic and ecological purposes.
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "single collection" 对应原文中的 "single nest"，而 "taxonomic and ecological purposes" 对应 "taxonomic investigations" 和 "ecological studies"。原文明确指出，这些方法并不总是兼容的，分类学家和生态学家有各自不同的需求，导致一个采集的方法不能同时满足两者的需求。因此，题干中的陈述是错误的，答案为 FALSE。

central_sentences.sentence: Unfortunately, these methods are not always compatible. The taxonomist sometimes overlooks whole species in favour of those groups currently under study, while the ecologist often collects only a limited number of specimens of each species, thus reducing their value for taxonomic investigations.
central_sentences.translation: 不幸的是，这些方法并不总是兼容的。分类学家有时会忽视整个物种，而偏爱当前正在研究的群体，而生态学家通常只收集每个物种的有限数量的标本，从而降低了它们在分类学研究中的价值。

target_sen.target_sen: Unfortunately, these methods are not always compatible.

The taxonomist sometimes overlooks whole species in favour of those groups currently under study, while the ecologist often collects only a limited number of specimens of each species, thus reducing their value for taxonomic investigations.
For ecological studies, the most important factor is collecting identifiable samples of as many of the different species present as possible.

groups.desc:
Questions 31-36
Choose the correct answer and move it into the gap.

questions.title: It is preferable to take specimens from groups of ants.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“take specimens from groups of ants”在原文中对应的表达是“collections should be made from nests or foraging columns and at least 20 to 25 individuals collected”。这段话明确指出了从巢穴或觅食队列中采集样本，并且采集的个体数量较多，这与题干的描述完全一致。因此，正确答案是A: hand collecting。

central_sentences.sentence: When possible, collections should be made from nests or foraging columns and at least 20 to 25 individuals collected.
central_sentences.translation: 如果可能的话，应该从蚁巢或觅食队列中采集样本，并至少收集20到25只个体。

target_sen.target_sen: When possible, collections should be made from nests or foraging columns and at least 20 to 25 individuals collected.
When possible, collections should be made from nests or foraging columns and at least 20 to 25 individuals collected.
When possible, collections should be made from nests or foraging columns and at least 20 to 25 individuals collected.

questions.title: It is particularly effective for wet habitats.
questions.analysis: 正确答案是 C. sampling ground litter，因为文章明确指出这种方法在雨林和沼泽地区（即潮湿的栖息地）特别有效。题干中的“潮湿的栖息地”与原文中的“rain forests”和“marshy areas”相对应。

central_sentences.sentence: This method works especially well in rain forests and marshy areas.
central_sentences.translation: 这种方法在雨林和沼泽地区特别有效。

target_sen.target_sen: This method works especially well in rain forests and marshy areas.

questions.title: It is a good method for species which are hard to find.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“good method for species which are hard to find”在原文中对应的表达是“attracts species that are otherwise elusive”。原文提到使用诱饵可以吸引那些难以找到的物种，因此答案是B: using bait。

central_sentences.sentence: Baits can be used to attract and concentrate foragers. This often increases the number of individuals collected and attracts species that are otherwise elusive.
central_sentences.translation: 诱饵可以用来吸引和集中觅食者。这通常会增加收集到的个体数量，并吸引那些原本难以捕捉的物种。

target_sen.target_sen: This often increases the number of individuals collected and attracts species that are otherwise elusive.
Baits can be used to attract and concentrate foragers.

questions.title: Little time and effort is required.
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是D. using a pitfall trap。题干中的“Little time and effort”意思是“需要很少的时间和精力”，在原文中对应的同义表达是“minimal maintenance and intervention”，这表明陷阱采集方法需要的时间和精力最少，因此答案是D。

central_sentences.sentence: One advantage of pitfall traps is that they can be used to collect over a period of time with minimal maintenance and intervention.
central_sentences.translation: 陷阱的一个优点是它们可以在一段时间内用于收集，只需要最少的维护和干预。

target_sen.target_sen: One advantage of pitfall traps is that they can be used to collect over a period of time with minimal maintenance and intervention.

questions.title: Separate containers are used for individual specimens.
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是A. hand collecting。题干中的“Separate containers are used for individual specimens”意思是“单独的容器用于单个标本”。在文章中，提到“individual insects are placed in plastic or glass tubes”，这表明每个标本是放在单独的容器中的。这个描述与题干意思完全相同，因此可以确定答案是A. hand collecting。

central_sentences.sentence: Individual insects are placed in plastic or glass tubes (1. 5-3. 0 ml capacity for small ants, 5-8 ml for larger ants) containing 75% to 95% ethanol
central_sentences.translation: 将单个昆虫放入装有75%至95%乙醇的塑料或玻璃管中（小蚂蚁使用1.5-3.0毫升容量的管，大蚂蚁使用5-8毫升的管）。

target_sen.target_sen: Individual insects are placed in plastic or glass tubes (1.
Individual insects are placed in plastic or glass tubes (1.

questions.title: Non-alcoholic preservative should be used.
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是D. using a pitfall trap。文章中明确提到在使用陷阱时，通常使用乙二醇或丙二醇作为防腐剂，因为酒精会很快蒸发，导致陷阱干涸。这与题干中的“Non-alcoholic preservative”直接对应。

central_sentences.sentence: The preservative used is usually ethylene glycol or propylene glycol, as alcohol will evaporate quickly and the traps will dry out.
central_sentences.translation: 通常使用的防腐剂是乙二醇或丙二醇，因为酒精会很快蒸发，导致陷阱干涸。

target_sen.target_sen: The preservative used is usually ethylene glycol or propylene glycol, as alcohol will evaporate quickly and the traps will dry out.

groups.title: One method of collecting ants
groups.desc:
Questions 37-40
Complete the table below.
Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the text in each gap.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“some 37 ....................”对应文章中的“often under some heat”。图片描述了一个收集蚂蚁的方法，其中提到落叶层在干燥过程中需要一些热量。通过关键词“some”和“heat”可以定位到答案句。因此，答案是“heat”。

central_sentences.sentence: This is most commonly done by placing leaf litter on a screen over a large funnel, often under some heat.
central_sentences.translation: 这通常是通过将落叶层放在一个大漏斗上的筛网上完成的，通常在一些热量下进行。

target_sen.target_sen: This is most commonly done by placing leaf litter on a screen over a large funnel, often under some heat.
This is most commonly done by placing leaf litter on a screen over a large funnel, often under some heat.

questions.analysis: 题目要求填空的部分是图片中漏斗上方的内容。通过题干中的“method of collecting ants”和图片中的“funnel”和“leaves”这两个线索词，可以定位到文章中描述这种方法的部分。文章中提到“ways to collect them”对应题干中的“method of collecting ants”，并且明确提到“leaf litter”是这种方法的一部分。因此，答案是“leaf litter”。学生需要注意的是，虽然图片中有叶子，但更准确的表达是“leaf litter”，因为这是文章中具体描述的方法。

central_sentences.sentence: One of the most successful ways to collect them is to gather the leaf litter in which they are foraging and extract the ants from it.
central_sentences.translation: 收集它们最成功的方法之一是收集它们觅食的落叶层，并从中提取蚂蚁。

target_sen.target_sen: One of the most successful ways to collect them is to gather the leaf litter in which they are foraging and extract the ants from it.
This is most commonly done by placing leaf litter on a screen over a large funnel, often under some heat.
As the leaf litter dries from above, ants (and other animals) move downward and eventually fall out the bottom and are collected in alcohol placed below the funnel.
A method of improving the catch when using a funnel is to sift the leaf litter through a coarse screen before placing it above the funnel.
It will also allow more litter to be sampled when using a limited number of funnels.
One of the most successful ways to collect them is to gather the leaf litter in which they are foraging and extract the ants from it.
This is most commonly done by placing leaf litter on a screen over a large funnel, often under some heat.
As the leaf litter dries from above, ants (and other animals) move downward and eventually fall out the bottom and are collected in alcohol placed below the funnel.
A method of improving the catch when using a funnel is to sift the leaf litter through a coarse screen before placing it above the funnel.

questions.analysis: 题目中的线索词是“a 39...”，结合图片可以看到39处是一个带孔的平面物体。文章中描述了一个类似的物体：“This is most commonly done by placing leaf litter on a screen over a large funnel, often under some heat.” 这里的“screen”与题干中的“a 39...”对应。学生如果词汇量有限，可以通过观察图片，联想到类似“筛子”或“网”的物体，并在文章中寻找与图片中物体形状和功能相似的描述。文章中提到“screen”时，描述了其放置在漏斗上方，并且用于筛选叶子和蚂蚁，这与图片中的物体功能一致。学生可能会错填“funnel”或“leaf litter”，但漏斗在图片中已经标注，不可能是答案；叶子垃圾是放在筛子上的物体，而不是筛子本身。因此，正确答案是“screen”。

central_sentences.sentence: This is most commonly done by placing leaf litter on a screen over a large funnel, often under some heat.
central_sentences.translation: 这通常是通过将叶子垃圾放在一个大漏斗上的筛子上完成的，通常在一些热量下进行。

target_sen.target_sen: This is most commonly done by placing leaf litter on a screen over a large funnel, often under some heat.
A method of improving the catch when using a funnel is to sift the leaf litter through a coarse screen before placing it above the funnel.

questions.analysis: 题目要求填空的部分是图片中的“40”，位于漏斗底部的液体中。我们需要在文章中找到描述这个液体的部分。通过定位关键词“funnel”，可以找到描述漏斗方法的段落。在这段话中，明确提到蚂蚁最终会掉到漏斗底部的液体中，而这个液体是“alcohol”。因此，正确答案是“alcohol”。学生容易错填“ethylene glycol”或“propylene glycol”，因为这些词出现在描述坑陷陷阱的方法中，而不是漏斗方法中。需要注意的是，题目中的线索词“funnel”在原文中直接对应“funnel”，而“40”对应“alcohol”。

central_sentences.sentence: As the leaf litter dries from above, ants (and other animals) move downward and eventually fall out the bottom and are collected in alcohol placed below the funnel.
central_sentences.translation: 随着上方的落叶层干燥，蚂蚁（和其他动物）会向下移动，最终从底部掉落，并被收集在漏斗下方的酒精中。

target_sen.target_sen: This is most commonly done by placing leaf litter on a screen over a large funnel, often under some heat.
As the leaf litter dries from above, ants (and other animals) move downward and eventually fall out the bottom and are collected in alcohol placed below the funnel.
A method of improving the catch when using a funnel is to sift the leaf litter through a coarse screen before placing it above the funnel.
It will also allow more litter to be sampled when using a limited number of funnels.
As the leaf litter dries from above, ants (and other animals) move downward and eventually fall out the bottom and are collected in alcohol placed below the funnel.
